title: nyc-commons — Party
description: The people, organizations, and agencies that recur on NYC records. Across the corpus these appear as PartyReference, PersonName, AgencyReference, OrganizationReference, ContactInfo/ContactPoint, VendorReference — the same handful of shapes re-declared per domain. Factored here so a person/agency reference means the same thing citywide.

PersonName.description: A person's name, decomposed the way agency systems store it.

ContactPoint.description: A way to reach a person or office — the shared shape behind the various ContactInfo/Contact/ContactPerson defs.

AgencyReference.description: A reference to a City agency (e.g. as it appears in Checkbook / procurement / budget data).

domain.description: This project's domain slug for the agency, e.g. 'dob' — links the reference back to its assessment.

OrganizationReference.description: A non-agency organization — a vendor, nonprofit provider, contractor, or business on a record.

identifier.description: Vendor ID / EIN / registration number where present.

PartyReference.description: A person or organization playing a role on a record — owner, applicant, respondent, permittee, provider. Generalizes the per-domain PartyReference/Respondent/Filer shapes.

role.description: The party's role on this record (Owner, Applicant, Respondent, Permittee, Provider, ...).
